question.text: Under SEBI (Debenture Trustees) Regulations, 1993, the recurring registration fee for a debenture trustee from the sixth year onwards is:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** A debenture trustee who has been granted a certificate of registration shall pay a fee of Rs. 9 lakh every three years from the sixth year, from the date of grant of certificate of registration. **The Trap:** Rs. 15 lakh is the recurring fee for credit rating agencies, and Rs. 20 lakh is the initial registration fee for debenture trustees -- these figures can be easily confused. **Cross-Exam Context:** Fee structures for intermediaries are commonly tested across NISM Series III-A and related exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The fee must be paid three months before the expiry of the block for which the previous fee was paid -- plan accordingly.
